assertionResults.details: Ablation study comparing activity-driven vs random pruning completed successfully. Activity-driven pruning achieved significantly better results: final loss of 9.23 vs 25.71 for random (64.1% improvement). Activity-driven model achieved perplexity of 8072.85 while random pruning resulted in infinite perplexity (numerical instability from poor pruning choices). This clearly demonstrates that activity-based weight selection outperforms random selection at the same sparsity level (~70-100%).
